name: White Mountain
method: Serves 6 MERINGUES 6 organic egg whites 250 g caster sugar pinch of salt 1 teaspoon white wine vinegar 1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract CHESTNUT BASE 600 g unsweetened chestnut purée 75 g caster sugar 50–100 ml cream TOPPING 12 meringues (see below) 250 ml cream 3 tablespoons caster sugar

Put the meringues into the preheated oven, turn the heat down to 120°C (gas mark ½ ) and cook them for about 30 minutes. Put a wooden spoon in the oven door to keep it slightly open and continue cooking the meringues for another hour. They should be crisp on the outside but still a little soft on the inside.
Transfer them to a rack and let them cool completely.
Mix the chestnut purée with the sugar, and add just enough cream so that the chestnut mass can be pushed through a potato ricer.
